prompt: Choose the sentence with correct subject-verb agreement.

choices.A: The list of supplies are on the counter.
choices.B: The list of supplies is on the counter.
choices.C: The list of supplies were on the counter.
choices.D: The list of supplies be on the counter.

explanation: The subject is singular: list. The main trap is A because supplies is plural, but it is part of a prepositional phrase and does not control the verb.

prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly uses a pronoun.

choices.A: Every student must bring their ID badge to orientation.
choices.B: Every student must bring his or her ID badge to orientation.
choices.C: Every student must bring them ID badge to orientation.
choices.D: Every student must bring your ID badge to orientation.

explanation: The singular subject every student takes a singular pronoun in standard formal usage. The main trap is A because their is common in speech but is less standard in this test-style item.

prompt: Which sentence uses the correct verb tense?

choices.A: Yesterday, the nurse explains the discharge plan to the patient.
choices.B: Yesterday, the nurse explain the discharge plan to the patient.
choices.C: Yesterday, the nurse explained the discharge plan to the patient.
choices.D: Yesterday, the nurse will explain the discharge plan to the patient.

explanation: Yesterday signals past time, so explained is correct. The main trap is A because explains is present tense.

prompt: Choose the sentence with correct punctuation.

choices.A: Before leaving the lab students must turn off the microscopes.
choices.B: Before leaving the lab, students must turn off the microscopes.
choices.C: Before leaving, the lab students must turn off the microscopes.
choices.D: Before leaving the lab students, must turn off the microscopes.

explanation: An introductory phrase should be followed by a comma here. The main trap is A because it omits the needed comma after lab.

prompt: Choose the sentence with the correct use of <u>its</u> or <u>it's</u>.

choices.A: The clinic updated it's schedule last week.
choices.B: The clinic updated its' schedule last week.
choices.C: The clinic updated its schedule last week.
choices.D: The clinic updated it is schedule last week.

explanation: Its is the possessive form; it's means it is. The main trap is A because it uses the contraction instead of the possessive.

prompt: Choose the sentence with correct capitalization.

choices.A: Our Biology class meets in the science building.
choices.B: Our biology Class meets in the science building.
choices.C: Our Biology Class meets in the Science Building.
choices.D: Our biology class meets in the science building.

explanation: General school subjects and generic building names are not capitalized. The main trap is A because Biology should not be capitalized in this context.

prompt:
Which choice corrects the fragment below?
<u>Because the lecture covered several difficult topics.</u>

choices.A: Because the lecture covered several difficult topics, many students took detailed notes.
choices.B: The lecture covered because several difficult topics.
choices.C: Several difficult topics because the lecture covered them.
choices.D: Because covered several difficult topics in the lecture.

explanation: This revision turns the fragment into a complete sentence. The main trap is B because it is still not grammatically correct.

prompt:
Choose the best revision to correct the run-on sentence.
<u>The storm arrived suddenly the campers packed their gear in a hurry.</u>

choices.A: The storm arrived suddenly, the campers packed their gear in a hurry.
choices.B: The storm arrived suddenly; the campers packed their gear in a hurry.
choices.C: The storm arrived suddenly the campers, packed their gear in a hurry.
choices.D: The storm arrived suddenly because the campers packed their gear in a hurry.

explanation: A semicolon correctly joins two related independent clauses. The main trap is A because a comma alone creates a comma splice.

prompt: Choose the sentence with the correct word.

choices.A: Their going to review the notes after class.
choices.B: There going to review the notes after class.
choices.C: They’re going to review the notes after class.
choices.D: Theyre going to review the notes after class.

explanation: They’re correctly means they are. The main trap is A because their is possessive, not a contraction.

prompt: Choose the sentence with correct punctuation.

choices.A: The patient asked, “When will the results be ready”?
choices.B: The patient asked, “When will the results be ready?”
choices.C: The patient asked “When will the results be ready?”
choices.D: The patient asked, “When will the results be ready”?

explanation: The question mark belongs inside the quotation marks because it is part of the quoted question. The main trap is D because it places the question mark outside the quotation.

prompt: Choose the sentence with the clearest pronoun reference.

choices.A: When Maya handed the form to Elena, she smiled.
choices.B: When Maya handed the form to Elena, Maya smiled.
choices.C: When Maya handed Elena's form to her, she smiled.
choices.D: Maya handed the form to Elena because she had finished it.

explanation: Repeating Maya removes the unclear pronoun reference. The main trap is A because she could refer to either Maya or Elena.

prompt: Choose the sentence with the correct possessive form.

choices.A: The nurses lounge is on the third floor.
choices.B: The nurse’s lounge is on the third floor.
choices.C: The nurses’ lounge is on the third floor.
choices.D: The nurse lounge’s is on the third floor.

explanation: The lounge belongs to multiple nurses, so the plural possessive nurses’ is correct. The main trap is B because nurse’s would mean one nurse owns the lounge.

prompt:
Choose the most concise revision.
<u>At this point in time, the committee is currently reviewing the proposal.</u>

choices.A: The committee is currently reviewing the proposal now.
choices.B: The committee is reviewing the proposal.
choices.C: At this point, the committee currently reviews the proposal.
choices.D: The committee, at this point in time, is reviewing the proposal currently.

explanation: This version removes unnecessary phrases like at this point in time and currently. The main trap is A because now and currently are redundant.

prompt: Which sentence is most appropriate for a formal email to an instructor?

choices.A: I can’t make it, so I’m gonna turn it in later.
choices.B: Sorry, but stuff came up and I need more time.
choices.C: I am requesting a one-day extension due to illness.
choices.D: Hey, I missed class, so send me what I need.

explanation: This choice is clear, respectful, and formal. The main trap is B because stuff came up is too informal for an instructor email.

prompt:
Choose the best transition to complete the sentence.
The road to the clinic was flooded; <u>_______</u>, several staff members arrived late.

choices.A: for example
choices.B: however
choices.C: as a result
choices.D: meanwhile

explanation: The second clause shows an effect of the flooded road, so as a result fits. The main trap is however because it signals contrast, not cause and effect.

prompt:
Choose the best order for the sentences below.
1. Finally, dry the equipment with a clean towel.
2. First, rinse the equipment with warm water.
3. Next, apply soap and scrub all surfaces.
explanation: First rinse, next scrub, and finally dry is the logical sequence. The main trap is A because it begins with finally, which cannot come first.

prompt: Choose the sentence that removes redundancy.

choices.A: The new innovation improved the check-in process.
choices.B: The new improvement improved the check-in process.
choices.C: The innovation improved the check-in process.
choices.D: The innovative innovation improved the check-in process.

explanation: Innovation already implies something new, so new innovation is redundant. The main trap is A because it keeps unnecessary repetition.

prompt: A paragraph explains how students can prepare for a difficult exam. Which sentence would be the best supporting detail?

choices.A: Many exams are offered during the school year.
choices.B: Students should review notes regularly instead of cramming the night before.
choices.C: Exams can make students feel nervous.
choices.D: Some students prefer blue pens to black pens.

explanation: This sentence directly supports a paragraph about exam preparation. The main trap is C because it is relevant but too general and less useful as advice.

prompt: Choose the best topic sentence for a paragraph about drinking enough water.

choices.A: Water is clear and found in many places.
choices.B: Staying hydrated can improve energy, focus, and physical comfort.
choices.C: There are many kinds of reusable bottles for sale.
choices.D: Some drinks are served with ice.

explanation: This sentence introduces the main idea clearly and broadly enough for a paragraph. The main trap is A because it is too general and unfocused.

prompt: Choose the best concluding sentence for a paragraph about volunteering at a food pantry.

choices.A: The pantry opens at 8:00 a.m. on Saturdays.
choices.B: Volunteers sort cans, stock shelves, and greet visitors.
choices.C: Community service can build practical skills while helping others.
choices.D: Some food pantries also accept soap and paper towels.

explanation: This sentence effectively wraps up the paragraph by stating a broader takeaway. The main trap is B because it is another detail, not a conclusion.

prompt:
Choose the most precise word to complete the sentence.
The researcher kept detailed notes to <u>_______</u> each change in the experiment.

choices.A: forget
choices.B: record
choices.C: imagine
choices.D: delay

explanation: Record means to write down or document changes accurately. The main trap is delay because it does not fit the sentence’s purpose.

prompt:
Which revision best matches a calm, professional tone?
<u>You need to fix this mistake right away.</u>

choices.A: Fix this mistake now.
choices.B: This mistake is unacceptable.
choices.C: Please correct this error as soon as possible.
choices.D: You really messed this up.

explanation: This revision is polite and professional while still direct. The main trap is A because it is too abrupt for a professional tone.

prompt:
Choose the best way to combine the sentences.
The tutoring center added evening hours. More students were able to attend.

choices.A: The tutoring center added evening hours, more students were able to attend.
choices.B: The tutoring center added evening hours, and more students were able to attend.
choices.C: Added evening hours the tutoring center and more students were able to attend.
choices.D: The tutoring center added evening hours because more students were able to attend it.

explanation: The coordinating conjunction and correctly joins the two related clauses. The main trap is A because it creates a comma splice.

prompt:
Choose the clearest revision.
<u>The article said the equipment was improved, which surprised the manager after reading it.</u>

choices.A: After reading the article, the manager was surprised that the equipment had been improved.
choices.B: The article said the equipment was improved after the manager read it.
choices.C: The manager, after reading it, surprised the article about the improved equipment.
choices.D: Reading it surprised the manager that the article improved the equipment.

explanation: This version clearly shows who read the article and what caused the surprise. The main trap is B because it suggests the equipment improved after the manager read the article.

prompt:
In the sentence below, what does the word <u>brief</u> most nearly mean?
The instructor gave a brief review before the test.

choices.A: expensive
choices.B: short
choices.C: confusing
choices.D: private

explanation: In context, brief means short in length or duration. The main trap is confusing because nothing in the sentence suggests difficulty understanding.

prompt:
Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
The hospital will <u>_______</u> a new safety policy next month.

choices.A: adapt
choices.B: implement
choices.C: imagine
choices.D: hesitate

explanation: Implement means put into effect, which fits a new policy. The main trap is adapt because the hospital is not adjusting the policy; it is introducing it.

prompt: Choose the correctly spelled word.

choices.A: independant
choices.B: independint
choices.C: independent
choices.D: indepedent

explanation: Independent is the correct spelling. The main trap is independant because it reflects a common spelling error.

prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly uses <u>affect</u> or <u>effect</u>.

choices.A: The new schedule may effect employee morale.
choices.B: The new schedule may affect employee morale.
choices.C: The new schedule may affects employee morale.
choices.D: The new schedule may effecting employee morale.

explanation: Affect is usually the verb meaning influence. The main trap is A because effect is usually a noun.

prompt: A receptionist is writing a reminder to a patient. Which sentence is most appropriate?

choices.A: Don’t forget your appointment tomorrow.
choices.B: You better show up tomorrow for your appointment.
choices.C: This is a reminder of your appointment tomorrow at 2:00 p.m.
choices.D: Remember tomorrow, okay?

explanation: This sentence is clear, courteous, and professional. The main trap is A because it is conversational rather than appropriately formal.

prompt:
Choose the best transition to complete the sentence.
The medication reduced the patient’s fever; <u>_______</u>, the cough remained severe.

choices.A: therefore
choices.B: similarly
choices.C: however
choices.D: for example

explanation: The sentence contrasts improvement in fever with continued severe cough, so however is the best transition. The main trap is therefore because it would incorrectly suggest cause and effect.

prompt:
Choose the sentence that presents the information most clearly.
The survey results were 18 students liked online classes, 7 preferred in-person classes, and 5 had no preference.

choices.A: Online classes were liked by 18 students, 7 preferred in-person classes, and 5 had no preference.
choices.B: The survey showed that 18 students liked online classes, 7 preferred in-person classes, and 5 had no preference.
choices.C: The survey results, liking online classes by 18 students, with 7 preferring in-person classes, and 5 had no preference.
choices.D: There was a survey, and 18 students liked online classes, and 7 preferred in-person classes, and 5 had no preference.

explanation: This version is the clearest and most grammatically complete. The main trap is D because repeating and weakens clarity and style.

prompt:
Choose the best paraphrase of the sentence below.
Original: Regular exercise can improve mood and sleep quality.

choices.A: Regular exercise is known to support better mood and sleep.
choices.B: Regular exercise can improve mood and sleep quality.
choices.C: Mood and sleep quality can improve because exercise mood sleep quality.
choices.D: Exercise, mood, and sleep quality are words used together.

explanation: This choice restates the meaning in new wording without copying the original too closely. The main trap is B because it repeats the original almost exactly.

prompt:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence.
The instructions were so <u>_______</u> that even first-time users were able to follow them.

choices.A: confusing
choices.B: vague
choices.C: clear
choices.D: lengthy

explanation: If first-time users could follow the instructions, clear is the best fit. The main trap is lengthy because long instructions are not necessarily easy to follow.

prompt:
Which revision creates the most effective sentence variety?
<u>The nurse checked the chart. The nurse reviewed the medication list. The nurse spoke with the patient.</u>

choices.A: The nurse checked the chart. The nurse reviewed the medication list. The nurse spoke with the patient.
choices.B: Checking the chart. Reviewing the medication list. Speaking with the patient.
choices.C: The nurse checked the chart, reviewed the medication list, and spoke with the patient.
choices.D: The chart was checked, the medication list was reviewed, and the patient was spoken with by the nurse.

explanation: This revision combines related actions smoothly and improves sentence variety. The main trap is D because it is grammatically correct but unnecessarily wordy and passive.

prompt: Choose the sentence in active voice.

choices.A: The forms were filed by the assistant.
choices.B: The assistant filed the forms.
choices.C: The forms had been filed by the assistant.
choices.D: The filing of the forms was completed by the assistant.

explanation: This sentence uses active voice because the subject performs the action directly. The main trap is A because it is passive voice.

prompt: Choose the sentence with the clearest wording.

choices.A: The technician told the patient that they needed to wait.
choices.B: The technician told the patient, “You need to wait.”
choices.C: The technician told them the patient needed to wait.
choices.D: The patient was told by the technician that they needed to wait by them.

explanation: This version is direct and avoids the unclear pronoun they. The main trap is A because they could refer to the technician or the patient.

prompt: A student is drafting a paragraph about time management. Which sentence best fits the paragraph’s purpose?

choices.A: My backpack is blue and has two side pockets.
choices.B: Using a planner can help students track deadlines and reduce stress.
choices.C: Many students eat lunch between classes.
choices.D: Some classrooms have large windows.

explanation: This sentence directly supports the paragraph’s focus on time management. The main trap is C because it mentions students but is unrelated to managing time.
